title: Remediate Capital One Attack Pattern
console_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Block S3 public access on affected buckets
2. IMMEDIATE: Enforce IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances
3. Review and restrict IAM role permissions (least privilege)
4. Restrict security group ingress to required ports only
6. Review CloudTrail logs for suspicious activity
7. Enable VPC Flow Logs for network visibility
8. Audit all cross-service IAM role trust policies
cli_command:
# Multi-step remediation:
# Step 1: Block S3 public access
aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket {BUCKET_NAME} --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true
# Step 2: Enforce IMDSv2
aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id {INSTANCE_ID} --http-tokens required --http-endpoint enabled
# Step 3: Remove open SG rules
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id {SG_ID} --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

references.title: S3 public access, SG restrictions, IMDSv2

rollback_difficulty: Hard — multiple services affected, test carefully
